generated_comment: Honestly in most homes it just works better when roles are divided practically. Husband handles finances, driving, outside work etc, wife manages kitchen and daily house stuff because usually women are naturally better at keeping the home running. Matlab har cheez ko sexism bolne ki zarurat nahi hai, jo jahan better ho wahi sambhale.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it naturalizes a gendered division of labor as 'practical' and frames women as inherently suited to domestic work while men handle finances and external tasks. It encodes efficiency framing and the 'he handles outside, she handles home' manifestation.
original_frame: Dress Code: Traditional Equal Respectable, Other Equal Questionable.
persona_text: You are Arjun. Age 26. Single. A man in Delhi. You are posting anonymously on a Facebook group called Delhi Confessions after seeing a girl in a short dress at a metro station who was getting stared at by men around her. You are writing your post about what you saw and giving your opinion on why girls who dress a certain way in public spaces are responsible for the attention they get and what they should expect.
